author: alico
highlighted_text: 31 year old male born at term via uncomplicated vaginal deliv- ery also with intellectual disability and psychomotor delay. At the age of 1 year, he developed seizures described as generalized tonic clonic shaking with left head turning lasting less than 2 min. He had not noted any parkinsonian symptoms or gait difficulty. Neuro- logical examination showed ideomotor apraxia, agraphesthesia, astereognosis, and bradykinesia without rigidity, rest tremor, or postural instability (Video patient 2). Reflexes were diminished e throughout with normal sensation (pinprick, vibration, and propri- oception) and inability to tandem walk (could be secondary to anti- epileptics). His MRI brain (Fig 1B) showed global atrophy of the ce- rebral hemispheres in addition to the cerebellar atrophy and thin- ning of the corpus callosum. A 20 min routine EEG was normal and two-dimensional echocardiogram showed mild concentric left ventricular hypertrophy. He continued to have seizures every other month despite

author: alico
highlighted_text: e treatment with levetiracetam, valproic acid, and lamotrigine.

author: alico
highlighted_text: 20 year old male who was born at full term via uncomplicated vaginal delivery without prenatal issues but global developmental delay and intellectual disability. At age18 he developed tremor and instability while walking leading to falls backwards. He also had drooling of saliva, slurred speech, illegible handwriting, and slow- ness in completion of daily tasks. There was no history of freezing of gait, REM sleep behavior disorder, or autonomic symptoms. Physical examination did not show dysmorphic features.

author: alico
highlighted_text: Neurological examination showed reduced eyelid blinking, hypo- mimia, hypophonia, bradykinesia (right more than left) in all four extremities, and rigidity. There was no rest or postural tremor but there was a mild action tremor during finger to nose testing. Gait was festinating, with reduced arm swing (right more than left), and en-bloc turning but he recovered unassisted on pull test. He also had ideomotor apraxia, agraphesthesia, and astereognosis (Video patient 1). Reflexes were diminished throughout with e normal sensation (pinprick, vibration, and proprioception), and bilateral extensor plantar response. His MRI brain (Fig 1A) showed mild cerebellar atrophy and thinning of the cerebellar folia. Levo- dopa was offered to the patient but at this time he chose to remain off treatment.

author: alico
highlighted_text: six nucleotides down stream of exon 2.

author: alico
highlighted_text: This genetic defect was present in both sib- lings (described above),

author: alico
highlighted_text: heterozygous in their unaffected mother and absent in two unaffected maternal uncles.
